pick.rationale: Duke enters as the undisputed #1 team in college basketball with a staggering +39.17 efficiency margin compared to Louisville's +25.42—a nearly 14-point disparity reflecting elite offensive production (4th nationally) combined with the nation's best defense (1st). The Blue Devils' dominant tournament run includes high-confidence victories over Kansas (82%) and Kansas State (87%), while Louisville's Sweet 16 win over UCLA came with only 65% confidence. Compounding Louisville's disadvantage, star guard Mikel Brown Jr. is ruled out for this Elite 8 matchup after missing the last four games with a back injury, significantly weakening their perimeter play and overall ceiling against Duke's system dominance.

reasoningStep.title: East Regional Final Analysis: Duke vs Louisville
reasoningStep.summary: Comprehensive evaluation of Elite 8 matchup using advanced metrics, tournament performance, and injury status
reasoningStep.evidence: Duke ranked #1 in both Torvik and KenPom systems with +39.17 efficiency margin, 4th-ranked offense (128 adjO), and 1st-ranked defense (90.4 adjD) on 31-2 record. Louisville ranked 16th/18th in Torvik/KenPom with +25.42 efficiency margin, 20-23rd ranked offense, 23rd-ranked defense on 23-10 record. Critical factor: Louisville star guard Mikel Brown Jr. ruled out vs Duke due to back injury after missing last 4 games. Duke's tournament confidence ratings (87%, 82%) significantly exceed Louisville's (65%). Rating gap of ~14 points in efficiency margin represents one of largest disparities in Elite 8 field.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: e8-east-1
Current game label: East Regional Final
Round: Elite 8
Resolved teams: Duke (duke) vs Louisville (louisville)
Prior committed picks: 60

I need to predict the winner of the East Regional Final (game ID: e8-east-1) between Duke and Louisville. This is an Elite 8 matchup.

Looking at the prior picks, I can see:
- Duke won s16-east-1 (Sweet 16) against Kansas with 82% confidence
- Louisville won s16-east-2 (Sweet 16) against UCLA with 65% confidence

Let me analyze what I know about these teams from the prior picks:

**Duke:**
- Ranked #1 in both Torvik and KenPom systems
- Efficiency margin: +39.17 (staggering)
- Offensive rating: 4th nationally at 128 adjO
- Defensive rating: 1st nationally at 90.4 adjD
- Record: 31-2
- Beat Kansas (who had +24.48 efficiency margin) with 82% confidence

**Louisville:**
- Ranked 16th in Torvik and 18th in KenPom
- Efficiency margin: +25.42
- Offensive rating: 124 adjO (top 25 nationally)
- Defensive rating: 98.1 adjD (23rd nationally)
- Record: 23-10
- Beat UCLA (who had +21.64 efficiency margin) with 65% confidence

The gap between these teams is significant:
- Duke's efficiency margin (+39.17) is nearly 14 points better than Louisville's (+25.42)
- Duke is ranked #1 vs Louisville at ~17th
- Duke's defense is elite (1st nationally) while Louisville's is good but not elite (23rd)
- Duke's offense is also elite (4th) vs Louisville's solid (top 25)

This is a clear mismatch on paper. Duke has been dominant throughout the tournament, winning their previous games with high confidence (87%, 82%). 

Louisville's Sweet 16 win came with only 65% confidence, suggesting they were more vulnerable. The data overwhelmingly favors Duke - they're the nation's best team by every metric, while Louisville is merely a solid top-20 program. In a single-elimination game, Duke's system dominance across all phases should prevail.

I should verify there aren't any injury concerns or recent news that might shift this assessment before finalizing my pick.

Great, I have the detailed ratings. Let me analyze:

**Duke:**
- Torvik Rank: 1, KenPom Rank: 1
- Record: 31-2
- AdjO: ~128 (4th nationally)
- AdjD: ~89-90 (1st nationally)
- AdjEM: +39.17

**Louisville:**
- Torvik Rank: 16, KenPom Rank: 18
- Record: 23-10
- AdjO: ~124 (20-23rd nationally)
- AdjD: ~98-99 (23rd nationally)
- AdjEM: +25.42

The gap is massive:
- Efficiency margin difference: ~13.75 points
- Duke's defense is elite (1st) vs Louisville's good but not elite (23rd)
- Duke's offense is elite (4th) vs Louisville's solid (20-23rd)
- Duke has won their tournament games with high confidence (87%, 82%)
- Louisville's Sweet 16 win was only 65% confidence

This is a clear mismatch. Duke is the #1 team in the country by every metric and has been dominant throughout the tournament. Louisville is a solid top-20 team but nowhere near Duke's level.

I should check for any injury news or recent developments that might affect this matchup before making my pick.

However, I notice there's some conflicting information:
- One result says "Louisville's Mikel Brown Jr. out vs. Duke due to back injury"
- Another result from Joe Lunardi's guide says "After missing the ACC tournament with a lower back injury, Louisville star freshman Mikel Brown Jr. should be ready to go for March Madness."

This suggests Brown was expected to return for the tournament but then got injured again or wasn't fully recovered. Either way, the most recent news indicates he's OUT for this Elite 8 matchup against Duke.

This is a major blow to Louisville. If they're missing their star guard in a crucial regional final, it significantly weakens their chances even further.

Combined with the massive rating disparity (Duke #1 vs Louisville ~17th, +39.17 efficiency margin vs +25.42), this makes Duke an overwhelming favorite.

Let me make my pick now.

3. **Offensive Edge**: Duke's offense is elite (4th) vs Louisville's good but not elite (20-23rd)
4. **Tournament Performance**: Duke won Sweet 16 with 82% confidence; Louisville only won with 65% confidence

This is an overwhelming favorite situation. Duke is the best team in the country by every metric, playing at home (or neutral site), healthy, while Louisville is missing their star player and was barely able to advance past UCLA in the Sweet 16.

My pick: Duke
